response:
1. **Function $ L(t) $ for the LED Light Output:**

The total illumination in the living room is the sum of the natural light $ N(t) $ and the LED light $ L(t) $. We want the total illumination to be exactly 500 lumens at all times. Therefore, we set up the equation:

$$
N(t) + L(t) = 500
$$

Solving for $ L(t) $:

$$
L(t) = 500 - N(t)
$$

Substitute the given function for $ N(t) $:

$$
L(t) = 500 - \left( 300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) \right)
$$

$$
L(t) = 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)
$$

This function ensures that the total illumination is always 500 lumens. The LED light output is adjusted to maintain the desired illumination level.

**Total Energy Consumption Over 24 Hours:**

The energy consumption is given by the rate of power consumption, which is 0.1 watts per lumen-hour. The total energy consumed is:

$$
\text{Energy} = \int_0^{24} L(t) \cdot 0.1 \, dt
$$

Substitute $ L(t) = 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) $:

$$
\text{Energy} = \int_0^{24} \left( 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) \right) \cdot 0.1 \, dt
$$

$$
\text{Energy} = 0.1 \int_0^{24} \left( 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) \right) \, dt
$$

$$
\text{Energy} = 0.1 \left[ 200t + 200 \cdot \frac{12}{\pi} \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) \right]_0^{24
